seed_problem: degree-bounded connected network design minimizing cost + lambda * spectral-radius (max node stress) surrogate
mutation: composite: degree-bounded spanning network design x robustness surrogate. The objective couples total build cost with a shortest-path-tree betweenness (max node stress under a unit multi-source relay flow to the hub). The min-cost tree is a fragile long chain (max stress ~ n-1); balancing needs a bushy, degree-bounded structure -> distinct strategies (min-cost Kruskal vs balanced BFS tree) genuinely diverge.
title: Interstellar Relay: Robust Degree-Bounded Backbone
strategies: trivial: emit the backbone chain (baseline, expensive + fragile)
greedy: minimum-cost degree-bounded spanning tree (Kruskal) -> low cost, high stress (chain trap)
strong: balanced breadth-first spanning tree from the hub -> bushy low-stress structure at moderate cost
local search / rerouting to co-minimize cost and the max-stress robustness surrogate
